322a59b7-e7b2-812c-b278-c4ea6b37b919.title: First Class Tech / Computech
322a59b7-e7b2-812c-b278-c4ea6b37b919.summary: Nicole Hills led a technical configuration call with Joshua Nugent (Rev.io engineer) and Jamal Beydoun (client admin) to address data migration blockers for Computech. The primary issues identified are severe data fragmentation (assets spread across 15+ tabs), inconsistent customer ID formats in Tigerpaw (4-7 digits), and incomplete Rev.io API permissions blocking access to critical modules (invoicing, timesheets, appointments, POs). The team confirmed Rev.io is replacing Halo PSA for billing and service management, but permission alignment is unresolved. Next steps include API permission verification, migration script adjustments, and enforcing a new onboarding policy requiring clean data or API access before project kickoff.
322a59b7-e7b2-812c-b278-c4ea6b37b919.actionItems:
• Joshua Nugent: Continue troubleshooting API permission limitations for invoices, timesheets, appointments, and POs in Rev.io; adjust main Tigerpaw migration script to handle customer ID variances; schedule weekly team collaboration meetings once API stabilizes
• Nicole Hills: Communicate to client the need to consolidate asset data into single lists; enforce new onboarding policy requiring API access or clean data sheets before kickoff; coordinate with Jamal and Joshua on permissions and data issues
• Jamal Beydoun: Verify and share full API permissions for Joshua covering Rev.io roles replacing Halo PSA; provide admin credentials for API testing in Halo and Rev.io environments; troubleshoot ticket and workflow data accessibility
322a59b7-e7b2-812c-b278-c4ea6b37b919.blockers: Multiple: (1) Client data fragmented across 15+ tabs with inconsistent column headers; (2) Customer IDs in Tigerpaw are sporadic (4-7 digits) requiring script adjustments; (3) Rev.io API permissions incomplete—missing access to invoices, timesheets, appointments, purchase orders, quotations; (4) Halo PSA being replaced by Rev.io but permission mapping unclear; (5) Large customer base (17,000 records) with custom statuses causing status mapping issues
322a59b7-e7b2-812c-b278-c4ea6b37b919.keyFinding: Client data is severely fragmented across multiple tabs with inconsistent customer ID formats (4-7 digits), and API permissions for Rev.io are incomplete, blocking comprehensive data extraction and migration progress.

322a59b7-e7b2-812c-b278-c4ea6b37b919.summary: This was a technical troubleshooting sync between Rev.io (Lauren Swygert) and JSI (Jennifer Torre), a tax compliance partner, regarding recurring tax total discrepancies between Rev.io and SureTax that are blocking Tricom's (Frank) filing deadline on Monday. The core issue is a Rev.io-SureTax API data mismatch that causes tax totals to diverge—unique to Rev.io among billing platforms—affecting at least two known clients (Frank/Tricom and Valtech) with a 2-year history. JSI is proceeding with Frank's filing using SureTax data despite the $40 discrepancy, while Lauren committed to engaging Rev.io's data engineering to explore adding service group information to Rev.io reports as a permanent solution to eliminate the SureTax dependency entirely.

- Lauren Swygert: Review previous communications from 2 years ago regarding tax discrepancies with SureTax for pattern analysis (owner: Lauren)
- Lauren Swygert: Work directly with SureTax to identify and fix the API/data mismatch causing $40 discrepancy in Frank's account (owner: Lauren + SureTax)
- Jennifer Torre: Proceed with Frank's tax data import and filing using current SureTax data despite $40 discrepancy to meet Monday deadline (owner: Jennifer)
- Jennifer Torre: Continue escalation of recurring tax discrepancy issues with SureTax team (owner: Jennifer)
- Both parties: Maintain streamlined communication to avoid multi-threaded confusion and coordinate improvements
322a59b7-e7b2-812c-b278-c4ea6b37b919.blockers: SureTax API discrepancy unresolved; Rev.io-SureTax integration issue is the root cause; Monday filing deadline for Frank creates time pressure; service group enhancement not yet implemented in Rev.io
322a59b7-e7b2-812c-b278-c4ea6b37b919.keyFinding: Rev.io has a systemic data integration issue with SureTax that causes tax total discrepancies unique to Rev.io (not seen with other billing platforms), and the root cause requires direct Rev.io-SureTax API coordination that JSI cannot resolve independently.

039428dd-ef49-4b05-9c84-50c4cf6f5c4b.summary: The team addressed a critical ACH file processing blocker: Rev.io's system produces unbalanced Nacha ACH files that the bank rejects, but the system cannot natively produce balanced files. JD used AI (Glean) to manually convert an unbalanced file to balanced format by adding offset accounts and correcting batch control records (changing header from '225' to '200'). Dan and Celine are testing this revised file for upload success. Parallel initiatives include billing address corrections for 8 invoices, TRX payment processing investigation, and RTC reporting user setup. The core issue remains unresolved: JD must consult leadership on whether the system can be updated to auto-produce balanced files or if this becomes an ongoing manual process.

• Dan Kearney: Test received ACH file using validator tool and confirm upload success; Retain original non-working ACH file and research third-party converter options (Treasury Software noted)
• Celine: Upload and test revised ACH file in system; Investigate bounced email addresses and update invalid contacts
• Andrew Millington: Assist with RTC reporting user account creation and permission settings; Enable collections analysis to run daily in afternoon; Delay activation of automated notices

039428dd-ef49-4b05-9c84-50c4cf6f5c4b.blockers: System architecture limitation: Rev.io cannot auto-generate balanced ACH files; bank may not accept unbalanced files; decision pending from Rev.io leadership on whether this requires professional services or can be produced as one-off report; client investigating if bank will accept unbalanced files as alternative
039428dd-ef49-4b05-9c84-50c4cf6f5c4b.keyFinding: Rev.io's system cannot natively produce balanced ACH files (produces unbalanced only), requiring manual workarounds via AI or third-party tools, with no clear path forward on system-level resolution without leadership decision on cost/feasibility.

131fd17b-f024-4869-a769-4aa09a42646a.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
131fd17b-f024-4869-a769-4aa09a42646a.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.

Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
131fd17b-f024-4869-a769-4aa09a42646a.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
131fd17b-f024-4869-a769-4aa09a42646a.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

143c07a2-0fba-4005-9fc3-b23f8bb09bc6.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
143c07a2-0fba-4005-9fc3-b23f8bb09bc6.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.

Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
143c07a2-0fba-4005-9fc3-b23f8bb09bc6.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
143c07a2-0fba-4005-9fc3-b23f8bb09bc6.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

2b32fce6-f3fd-4e42-a922-44fb8195b2e9.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
2b32fce6-f3fd-4e42-a922-44fb8195b2e9.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
2b32fce6-f3fd-4e42-a922-44fb8195b2e9.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
2b32fce6-f3fd-4e42-a922-44fb8195b2e9.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
2b32fce6-f3fd-4e42-a922-44fb8195b2e9.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

DFW Security introduced their Inventory Manager (Bagley) and will serve as the pilot customer for Rev.io's new Sedona API integration—a first-time effort requiring Josh (Director of Data Engineers) to build extraction capability. The team identified data cleanup work needed (merging duplicate customer records across Sedona and Account Tools), discussed 31 inventory locations for warehouse and fleet vehicles, reviewed quote/invoice templates, and encountered MFA authentication issues that were partially resolved. A follow-up session is scheduled for Thursday to continue inventory management deep dive and address login issues.
2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.actionItems:
• Nicole: Ping Josh to join call and explain data extraction/API processes (04:34); continue quote/invoice templates with legal text and dynamic fields (11:10); investigate AR reporting for credits/revenue recognition (47:48); forward Sedona integration docs from Josh to Tyler (53:41); adjust MFA to phone numbers and collect DFW phone numbers (01:04:42); coordinate with product team on SKU field visibility timeline (34:23); follow up on login issues and schedule Thursday deep dive inventory session (01:09:40)
• Josh: Set up admin access for Sedona remote desktop and prepare API credentials (57:31); review and test extraction of customers, products, customer history from Sedona (59:23); engage with Nicole/team on API key building and data pull details (01:01:15)
• Tyler Morton: Provide phone numbers to Nicole for authentication update (01:05:27); stay engaged with Josh/Nicole on import status and data mapping (01:01:25)
• DFW Security: Set up 31 inventory locations (warehouse + ~30 vans/trucks) (50:36); review products vs assets usage and confirm inventory tagging/serial tracking (24:32); continue Sedona data cleanup to merge duplicate customer records before import (06:10)
2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.blockers: DFW Security is first Sedona integration pilot—Josh must build extraction capability from scratch; customer data mismatch between Sedona and Account Tools requires pre-import cleanup; MFA authentication loop delaying session depth; SKU field not yet visible in product catalog UI (workaround using UPC field); login issues postponed deeper inventory management training to Thursday
2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.keyFinding: DFW Security will be the first Sedona integration pilot for Rev.io's new API-based data extraction process, introducing technical complexity and dependency risk on Josh's ability to execute.

2f1a59b7-e7b2-8058-836a-c150b2e6f514.summary: Knight Security discussed a phased 12-month data migration strategy focused on moving ~100 recurring revenue customers monthly from their Premiere system to Rev.io. 2fba59b7-e7b2-80a5-81f7-d8c725b70b09.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
2fba59b7-e7b2-80a5-81f7-d8c725b70b09.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
2fba59b7-e7b2-80a5-81f7-d8c725b70b09.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle

2fca59b7-e7b2-805e-ba3f-e7bbe5621163.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
2fca59b7-e7b2-805e-ba3f-e7bbe5621163.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
2fca59b7-e7b2-805e-ba3f-e7bbe5621163.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
2fca59b7-e7b2-805e-ba3f-e7bbe5621163.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
2fca59b7-e7b2-805e-ba3f-e7bbe5621163.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

The team identified data cleanup work needed (merging duplicate customer records across Sedona and Account Tools), discussed 31 inventory locations for warehouse and fleet vehicles, reviewed quote/invoice templates, and encountered MFA authentication issues that were partially resolved. A follow-up session is scheduled for Thursday to continue inventory management deep dive and address login issues.

306a59b7-e7b2-80d5-b554-f326b26c39d5.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
306a59b7-e7b2-80d5-b554-f326b26c39d5.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
306a59b7-e7b2-80d5-b554-f326b26c39d5.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
306a59b7-e7b2-80d5-b554-f326b26c39d5.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
306a59b7-e7b2-80d5-b554-f326b26c39d5.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

306a59b7-e7b2-80d5-b554-f326b26c39d5.summary: Fire Team & Security is in the Configuration stage of PSA onboarding. The call focused heavily on data migration strategy from Tiger Paw, with Kegan explaining that manual cleanup of inactive/legacy customers and products is required before a SQL database backup can be pulled. The team reviewed quoting templates, ticketing workflows, time logging, and mobile app features. Key decision: client will import customers and tickets from Tiger Paw (rather than QuickBooks) to avoid manual account ID matching, but must first clean up Tiger Paw data to prevent importing obsolete accounts. Phased approach agreed: import active customers with recurring services first, then inactive customers later. Multiple action items assigned to Cheris, Billy, and Roxy for data cleanup and training.

• Billy Gaglione: Pull customer export report from Tiger Paw and share with team for review and cleanup; prepare cleaned CSV files following Rev.io import template; review and adjust Tiger Paw export filters for active/inactive customers and rep assignments; coordinate data export specifics and forward to Kegan for import

306a59b7-e7b2-80d5-b554-f326b26c39d5.blockers: Client is on-premise Tiger Paw (not hosted/Unleashed), requiring SQL database backup instead of simple CSV export. Full ticket import requires manual matching of accounts to tickets if importing from QuickBooks instead of Tiger Paw. Customer and product data cleanup is time-intensive and must be completed before database backup to avoid importing legacy/dead accounts. Quotes from Tiger Paw will not transfer; clients must reference QuickBooks for historical quote lookup.

317a59b7-e7b2-803c-92bd-ed371dbb2b61.summary: Halle Taylor conducted a deep-dive configuration call on inventory management and purchasing workflows. The client (Stephanie Kern and team) demonstrated their existing three-step ConnectWise process and exposed a critical incompatibility: Rev PSA does not allow adding non-ticket-linked products to a single purchase order—a core workflow in Losh's operation. Stephanie flagged this as a potential deal-breaker. Multiple additional gaps were identified: no unified purchasing approval dashboard, product catalog configuration blocked by a bug, missing serial number bulk upload workflow, no subcontractor system integration, and several in-development features not yet available. While client engagement is strong and next steps are clear, momentum is stalled by technical blockers and feature gaps that may jeopardize the transition from ConnectWise.

317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.title: Fuse Cloud
317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.summary: Fuse Cloud's 119-minute configuration call covered ticketing workflow, inventory management, quoting, contracts, and project management. Mark Kephart walked through ticket creation, filtering, and dispatch functionality while Dustin Lassiter identified critical usability gaps: cascading subtype dropdowns for 40+ subtypes across 1500 monthly tickets, complex filtering workarounds for 30 users, and mandatory multiple inbox feature (blocked until Aug 1). Client has basic email ingestion working but outgoing email requires DNS validation. Approximately 18 feature requests and improvements logged; next weekly meetings scheduled. Client at Configuration stage with clear blockers preventing productive use of the system.
317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.actionItems: **Mark Kephart**: (1) Accelerate multiple inbox rollout ahead of August 1 timeline—client marked as blocker; (2) Follow up on ticket status/subtype renaming and coloring options via email from Dustin; (3) Investigate cascading subtype dropdowns per ticket type (critical for 1500 monthly tickets); (4) Explore advanced filtering for assigned/unassigned tickets using user groups or operators to avoid creating 70+ saved views; (5) Confirm timeline for adding multiple contacts directly in tickets; (6) Investigate macro editing after application; (7) Explore journal/time logging integration; (8) Test calendar quick view bug; (9) Investigate saved default filter views on dispatch board; (10) Create reports for dispatched vs. non-dispatched tickets; (11) Assign tax codes to product types; (12) Confirm workflow automation for status changes on client responses; (13) Coordinate role permissions for project management; (14) Verify milestone notification automation; (15) Assist with project archiving/templates; (16) Schedule recurring weekly meetings 3–5 PM ET / 2–4 PM CT. **Dustin Lassiter**: Submit detailed ticket status/subtype change requests via email.
317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.blockers: CRITICAL: Multiple inbox functionality (estimated Aug 1 launch); Cascading subtype dropdowns not yet supported (40+ subtypes must use flat list); Ticket filtering complexity requires workaround for large teams (70+ saved views for 30 users); Macro editing not possible after application; Email outgoing validation blocked (DNS tilde record for .cloud domain); Ad hoc contact addition on ticket creation not yet available.
317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.keyFinding: Client identified multiple inbox functionality as a critical blocker—they stated the system is 'essentially impossible to use' without it, and it won't ship until August 1st at the earliest.

317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.title: Fuse.Cloud
317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.summary: Fuse.Cloud participated in a 128-minute PSA configuration call covering user roles, ticket queue management, notifications, inventory tracking, and sales pipeline integration. Mark Kephart walked through foundational PSA setup, but significant friction emerged when Dustin challenged whether PSA's single-queue, filter-based architecture adequately supports their multi-product-line operations (Voice, Circuit, Fax, IT L2/L3 queues). While Mark outlined workarounds using ticket types and saved filters, the client expressed clear frustration that this differs from their Billing system model and may not scale to their complexity. Configuration of ticket types and user groups was deferred to next call; next session will focus on ticketing and quoting deep-dive training.
317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.actionItems:
• Dustin Lassiter: Build out ticket types and user groups in PSA before next call to match Fuse.Cloud's multi-queue structure (02:06:50)
• Walter: Manage Microsoft Entra and DNS record setup for support inbox email address verification (32:10)
• Mark Kephart: Investigate PSA's support for multiple outgoing email addresses and submit feature request (41:59); Add labor product catalog item with time entry reasons and rounding rules (43:30); Send meeting invites for next PSA training session focused on ticketing and quoting (02:05:50)
• Jeremy Adams: Verify default outgoing email reply behavior when no outgoing email is configured (01:06:00)
• Unassigned: Continue exploring inventory management limitations and workarounds for direct customer inventory transfer without tickets (01:55:30)
317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.blockers: PSA architectural limitation: single ticket queue with filter-based views does not provide discrete queue separation that client needs for multi-product-line operations (Voice, Circuit, Fax, IT L2/L3). User groups control notifications only, not visibility/filtering. Only one outgoing email address supported, limiting multi-mailbox support inbox flexibility. Inventory cannot be directly transferred to customers without creating tickets.
317a59b7-e7b2-80aa-9578-fccfc9cfddef.keyFinding: Client has complex multi-queue, multi-product-line requirements (Voice, Circuit, Fax, IT L2/L3) that PSA's single-queue architecture with filter-based organization does not naturally support, creating architectural misalignment and frustration.

The team discovered that the Rev.io system misclassifies admin time and PTO in utilization reports, lacks proper labor code and billable rate configuration, has an unreliable mobile app, and is missing core functionality needed for accurate billing and payroll. Jason and Kathy requested simplification of time entry reasons and clarification on how the system tracks customer-billable vs. non-billable time, while Halle agreed to escalate product concerns, negotiate licensing credits, and coordinate QuoteWorks integration discussions.

32fa59b7-e7b2-81e5-8e18-c000766e6d83.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
32fa59b7-e7b2-81e5-8e18-c000766e6d83.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.

Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work

32fa59b7-e7b2-81e5-8e18-c000766e6d83.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

The team reviewed the current migration state (1,800 customers from billing system only) and decided to hold Tiger Paw data migration pending scope confirmation due to duplication risks and the need to filter 4,700+ accounts. Critical data import templates were shared, outlining required fields (account ID, parent ID, address) and optional fields for contacts, products, ticket history, and recurring services. The ticketing module was demonstrated with macro automation, time logging, dispatch board integration, and customer portal capabilities, with action items assigned to define final scope and send templates.

334a59b7-e7b2-8144-930d-e240ed90a1fc.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
334a59b7-e7b2-8144-930d-e240ed90a1fc.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
334a59b7-e7b2-8144-930d-e240ed90a1fc.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
334a59b7-e7b2-8144-930d-e240ed90a1fc.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
334a59b7-e7b2-8144-930d-e240ed90a1fc.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

334a59b7-e7b2-8144-930d-e240ed90a1fc.summary: Fire Team & Security is in the Configuration stage of PSA onboarding. The team reviewed quoting templates, ticketing workflows, time logging, and mobile app features. Key decision: client will import customers and tickets from Tiger Paw (rather than QuickBooks) to avoid manual account ID matching, but must first clean up Tiger Paw data to prevent importing obsolete accounts. Phased approach agreed: import active customers with recurring services first, then inactive customers later. Multiple action items assigned to Cheris, Billy, and Roxy for data cleanup and training.

• Billy Gaglione: Pull customer export report from Tiger Paw and share with team for review and cleanup; prepare cleaned CSV files following Rev.io import template; review and adjust Tiger Paw export filters for active/inactive customers and rep assignments; coordinate data export specifics and forward to Kegan for import
• Implementation Team (Cheris, Billy, Roxy): Practice creating, sending, and managing quotes; familiarize with ticket management (notes, signatures, parts/labor, dispatching via desktop and mobile)

Quotes from Tiger Paw will not transfer; clients must reference QuickBooks for historical quote lookup.

337a59b7-e7b2-800f-961e-dee18b068878.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
337a59b7-e7b2-800f-961e-dee18b068878.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
337a59b7-e7b2-800f-961e-dee18b068878.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
337a59b7-e7b2-800f-961e-dee18b068878.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
337a59b7-e7b2-800f-961e-dee18b068878.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

The call focused heavily on data migration strategy from Tiger Paw, with Kegan explaining that manual cleanup of inactive/legacy customers and products is required before a SQL database backup can be pulled. The team reviewed quoting templates, ticketing workflows, time logging, and mobile app features. Key decision: client will import customers and tickets from Tiger Paw (rather than QuickBooks) to avoid manual account ID matching, but must first clean up Tiger Paw data to prevent importing obsolete accounts. Phased approach agreed: import active customers with recurring services first, then inactive customers later. Multiple action items assigned to Cheris, Billy, and Roxy for data cleanup and training.

• James and Sean: Coordinate with client to prepare Tiger Paw SQL database backup for customer and product data extraction
• Cheris Campbell and Billy Gaglione: Prioritize and clean up current customers with recurring agreements/monitoring services for initial import; consolidate product SKUs (e.g., wiring products); continue cleaning customer and product data to remove duplicates and outdated data
• Billy Gaglione: Pull customer export report from Tiger Paw and share with team for review and cleanup; prepare cleaned CSV files following Rev.io import template; review and adjust Tiger Paw export filters for active/inactive customers and rep assignments; coordinate data export specifics and forward to Kegan for import
• Implementation Team (Cheris, Billy, Roxy): Practice creating, sending, and managing quotes; familiarize with ticket management (notes, signatures, parts/labor, dispatching via desktop and mobile)

337a59b7-e7b2-800f-961e-dee18b068878.blockers: Client is on-premise Tiger Paw (not hosted/Unleashed), requiring SQL database backup instead of simple CSV export. Full ticket import requires manual matching of accounts to tickets if importing from QuickBooks instead of Tiger Paw. Customer and product data cleanup is time-intensive and must be completed before database backup to avoid importing legacy/dead accounts. Quotes from Tiger Paw will not transfer; clients must reference QuickBooks for historical quote lookup.

The team confirmed Rev.io is replacing Halo PSA for billing and service management, but permission alignment is unresolved. Next steps include API permission verification, migration script adjustments, and enforcing a new onboarding policy requiring clean data or API access before project kickoff.

345a59b7-e7b2-8124-a213-c551e65b4ad9.title: VIA Technology
345a59b7-e7b2-8124-a213-c551e65b4ad9.summary: Nicole Hills led a comprehensive 108-minute PSA configuration discovery call with VIA Technology, covering product catalog setup, billing workflows, quote templates, ticketing, labor tracking, purchase orders, calendar/task management, and mobile geofencing. The team streamlined VIA's QuickBooks product list into generic billing categories (labor, materials, freight) and clarified that detailed project billing occurs in Textura with summary revenue rolled into PSA and QB monthly. A key tension emerged: VIA wants to shift from percentage-complete labor billing to hourly time tracking, but this conflicts with their Textura-driven workflow. Multiple configuration workstreams were assigned to Nicole (templates, billing rules, workflows, geofence, training), and vendor/provider data is pending from Patty. Client is engaged and prepared, but lacks hands-on PSA experience and clear resolution on the billing model transition.

• Nicole Hills: Enter/coordinate product list entry into PSA (12:05); create quote templates for quick service jobs and detailed project scopes (18:00); configure taxable/non-taxable billing with tax percentages and finance charges (13:30); set up ticket status workflow notifications for billing/PM alerts (44:00); implement role/permission controls for tech note and pricing modifications (42:30); configure labor categories with time entry reasons and rounding rules (56:00); set up geofence and travel timer for mobile clock-ins (01:40:00); create task/calendar reminders for PMs and techs (01:22:00); provide tech training on note-taking and ticketing (48:10); schedule recurring weekly PSA config calls (01:44:20)
• Mike: Review hybrid time tracking strategy (ticket-level logs + clock in/out) with Nicole (01:05:40)
345a59b7-e7b2-8124-a213-c551e65b4ad9.blockers: No direct integration with Textura system; manual monthly billing reconciliation required. 345a59b7-e7b2-8124-a213-c551e65b4ad9.keyFinding: VIA Technology's billing workflow is tightly coupled to Textura (external AIA/pay application system) with no current integration, requiring manual monthly reconciliation of labor and materials into PSA—this creates a potential bottleneck for implementing hourly labor tracking as desired.

349a59b7-e7b2-81f8-88e9-cd925ebe75d4.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
349a59b7-e7b2-81f8-88e9-cd925ebe75d4.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
349a59b7-e7b2-81f8-88e9-cd925ebe75d4.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
349a59b7-e7b2-81f8-88e9-cd925ebe75d4.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
349a59b7-e7b2-81f8-88e9-cd925ebe75d4.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

Phased approach agreed: import active customers with recurring services first, then inactive customers later. Multiple action items assigned to Cheris, Billy, and Roxy for data cleanup and training.

34aa59b7-e7b2-80d1-9de6-c693fee3c71a.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
34aa59b7-e7b2-80d1-9de6-c693fee3c71a.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
34aa59b7-e7b2-80d1-9de6-c693fee3c71a.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
34aa59b7-e7b2-80d1-9de6-c693fee3c71a.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
34aa59b7-e7b2-80d1-9de6-c693fee3c71a.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

34aa59b7-e7b2-81dd-8801-c5a6224b23e2.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
34aa59b7-e7b2-81dd-8801-c5a6224b23e2.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
34aa59b7-e7b2-81dd-8801-c5a6224b23e2.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
34aa59b7-e7b2-81dd-8801-c5a6224b23e2.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
34aa59b7-e7b2-81dd-8801-c5a6224b23e2.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.summary: Fire Team & Security is in the Configuration stage of PSA onboarding. The call focused heavily on data migration strategy from Tiger Paw, with Kegan explaining that manual cleanup of inactive/legacy customers and products is required before a SQL database backup can be pulled. The team reviewed quoting templates, ticketing workflows, time logging, and mobile app features. Key decision: client will import customers and tickets from Tiger Paw (rather than QuickBooks) to avoid manual account ID matching, but must first clean up Tiger Paw data to prevent importing obsolete accounts. Phased approach agreed: import active customers with recurring services first, then inactive customers later. Multiple action items assigned to Cheris, Billy, and Roxy for data cleanup and training.
351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.actionItems:
• James and Sean: Coordinate with client to prepare Tiger Paw SQL database backup for customer and product data extraction
• Cheris Campbell and Billy Gaglione: Prioritize and clean up current customers with recurring agreements/monitoring services for initial import; consolidate product SKUs (e.g., wiring products); continue cleaning customer and product data to remove duplicates and outdated data
• Billy Gaglione: Pull customer export report from Tiger Paw and share with team for review and cleanup; prepare cleaned CSV files following Rev.io import template; review and adjust Tiger Paw export filters for active/inactive customers and rep assignments; coordinate data export specifics and forward to Kegan for import
• Implementation Team (Cheris, Billy, Roxy): Practice creating, sending, and managing quotes; familiarize with ticket management (notes, signatures, parts/labor, dispatching via desktop and mobile)
• Roxy Rich and Technical Staff: Train users (e.g., Dylan) on entering time logs with proper log reasons for accurate billing rates
• Kegan Ehlers: Assist with template compliance and import data uploads once clean files received
351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.blockers: Client is on-premise Tiger Paw (not hosted/Unleashed), requiring SQL database backup instead of simple CSV export. Full ticket import requires manual matching of accounts to tickets if importing from QuickBooks instead of Tiger Paw. Customer and product data cleanup is time-intensive and must be completed before database backup to avoid importing legacy/dead accounts. Quotes from Tiger Paw will not transfer; clients must reference QuickBooks for historical quote lookup.
351a59b7-e7b2-8105-9dc0-e8e71fd986cb.keyFinding: Client must clean up Tiger Paw customer and product data before database backup and import, which is a time-intensive manual process that could delay go-live.

3a30ef69-2cf1-414f-a7e1-b07eb4d9f47f.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
3a30ef69-2cf1-414f-a7e1-b07eb4d9f47f.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
3a30ef69-2cf1-414f-a7e1-b07eb4d9f47f.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
3a30ef69-2cf1-414f-a7e1-b07eb4d9f47f.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
3a30ef69-2cf1-414f-a7e1-b07eb4d9f47f.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

No API calls are reaching Rev.io's system logs, indicating a potential integration failure at the client's on-premise infrastructure level. The team has identified the need for client database dumps to extract transaction GUIDs and trace the root cause; once obtained, Sean and Diana expect to quickly pinpoint the input format error preventing card data from being saved.
3a30ef69-2cf1-414f-a7e1-b07eb4d9f47f.actionItems:
Kegan Ehlers: Obtain on-premise database dump containing payment method data from client/third-party IT immediately
Kegan Ehlers: Extract reference GUIDs from test transaction attempts in database and provide to Sean and Diana
Sean Kuta: Analyze database data and reference GUIDs to identify source of card data saving errors in pay_methods table
Diana Collins: Cross-reference API call GUIDs in Rev.io system logs to identify response errors and confirm whether calls are reaching the payment system
3a30ef69-2cf1-414f-a7e1-b07eb4d9f47f.blockers: On-premise architecture blocks direct access to client database and logs; no API calls visible in Rev.io logs indicating requests never reached the system; test card submissions failing with 'input string was not in correct format' error; client requires third-party IT involvement to extract database dumps
3a30ef69-2cf1-414f-a7e1-b07eb4d9f47f.keyFinding: Payment system integration completely blocked: API calls not reaching Rev.io systems and test card validation failing due to input format errors; on-premise architecture requires client database dump to proceed with troubleshooting.

5350814e-0e68-48ce-ab1e-af86b0415ed0.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
5350814e-0e68-48ce-ab1e-af86b0415ed0.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
5350814e-0e68-48ce-ab1e-af86b0415ed0.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
5350814e-0e68-48ce-ab1e-af86b0415ed0.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
5350814e-0e68-48ce-ab1e-af86b0415ed0.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

5350814e-0e68-48ce-ab1e-af86b0415ed0.title: Viking Lock
5350814e-0e68-48ce-ab1e-af86b0415ed0.summary: Nicole Hills conducted a 107-minute configuration call covering deposit feature introduction (launching Thursday), billing profile setup, walk-in customer tracking for point-of-sale, inventory management, permissions/accountability controls, and QuickBooks Desktop integration status. Viking Lock expressed interest in the new deposit feature and confirmed preferences for Google Calendar integration, but critical action items—billing profile creation and staff training—remain incomplete. 8686391a-7a75-4534-9ea5-387202eb228f.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
8686391a-7a75-4534-9ea5-387202eb228f.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
8686391a-7a75-4534-9ea5-387202eb228f.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
8686391a-7a75-4534-9ea5-387202eb228f.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
8686391a-7a75-4534-9ea5-387202eb228f.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

8cd8990f-f0a7-412c-9813-6831795fa4d0.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
8cd8990f-f0a7-412c-9813-6831795fa4d0.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
8cd8990f-f0a7-412c-9813-6831795fa4d0.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
8cd8990f-f0a7-412c-9813-6831795fa4d0.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
8cd8990f-f0a7-412c-9813-6831795fa4d0.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

abab712e-6733-4648-9deb-28cbd16b00d6.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
abab712e-6733-4648-9deb-28cbd16b00d6.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
abab712e-6733-4648-9deb-28cbd16b00d6.actionItems:
Heavenly Burdette: Share spreadsheet of affected customers and begin testing reversal/credit process on one customer this afternoon (today)
Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work
abab712e-6733-4648-9deb-28cbd16b00d6.blockers: Internal ticket submission required for area code logic fix; depends on Rev.io internal engineering team to implement change before next billing cycle
abab712e-6733-4648-9deb-28cbd16b00d6.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

b4d1e7b5-e04c-4660-a84f-1d27406be1c3.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
b4d1e7b5-e04c-4660-a84f-1d27406be1c3.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.
b4d1e7b5-e04c-4660-a84f-1d27406be1c3.actionItems:

cae103a8-b3f0-4cbe-894c-44b5532fc9c6.title: RIT (Rite Telephone)
cae103a8-b3f0-4cbe-894c-44b5532fc9c6.summary: This was a quick remediation call (not a standard onboarding call) addressing a critical billing defect discovered post-implementation. RIT's legacy homegrown PSA system had issues migrating seven-digit phone numbers and zero-duration calls into Rev.io, creating overcharges on ~360 customers. The team designed a pragmatic hybrid fix: issue credits (no bill reversal) for discrepancies under $10 and $5 respectively, and manually reverse/re-rate bills for larger amounts to preserve ConnectWise integration integrity. Testing begins today with full execution tomorrow, and a permanent code fix is being submitted internally.

Heavenly Burdette: Perform bulk credit creation for customers below $10 threshold (seven-digit) and below $5 threshold (zero-duration calls) by tomorrow
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Manually reverse bills and re-rate usage for customers exceeding thresholds starting tomorrow; estimate 1-2 hours for ~30 customers
Lex Ligon: Submit internal ticket to update billing logic to prepend area codes to CERs to prevent future seven-digit issues in next billing cycle
Rob Hiller: Run comparative review on billing data once corrections are underway to validate accuracy before sending corrected invoices to customers
Heavenly Burdette & Lex Ligon: Coordinate progress updates during reversal process; Heavenly to notify Lex when ready to proceed with bulk work

cae103a8-b3f0-4cbe-894c-44b5532fc9c6.keyFinding: RIT discovered a seven-digit phone number billing error affecting ~360 customers; the team established a hybrid remediation plan using $10 and $5 thresholds to minimize manual work and ConnectWise integration complications.

The call focused heavily on data migration strategy from Tiger Paw, with Kegan explaining that manual cleanup of inactive/legacy customers and products is required before a SQL database backup can be pulled. The team reviewed quoting templates, ticketing workflows, time logging, and mobile app features. Key decision: client will import customers and tickets from Tiger Paw (rather than QuickBooks) to avoid manual account ID matching, but must first clean up Tiger Paw data to prevent importing obsolete accounts. Phased approach agreed: import active customers with recurring services first, then inactive customers later. Multiple action items assigned to Cheris, Billy, and Roxy for data cleanup and training.

• Billy Gaglione: Pull customer export report from Tiger Paw and share with team for review and cleanup; prepare cleaned CSV files following Rev.io import template; review and adjust Tiger Paw export filters for active/inactive customers and rep assignments; coordinate data export specifics and forward to Kegan for import

Customer and product data cleanup is time-intensive and must be completed before database backup to avoid importing legacy/dead accounts. Quotes from Tiger Paw will not transfer; clients must reference QuickBooks for historical quote lookup.

cae103a8-b3f0-4cbe-894c-44b5532fc9c6.summary: Fire Team & Security encountered critical payment system integration failures during configuration, with test card submissions blocking profile creation and validation. No API calls are reaching Rev.io's system logs, indicating a potential integration failure at the client's on-premise infrastructure level. The team has identified the need for client database dumps to extract transaction GUIDs and trace the root cause; once obtained, Sean and Diana expect to quickly pinpoint the input format error preventing card data from being saved.
cae103a8-b3f0-4cbe-894c-44b5532fc9c6.actionItems:

Diana Collins: Cross-reference API call GUIDs in Rev.io system logs to identify response errors and confirm whether calls are reaching the payment system
cae103a8-b3f0-4cbe-894c-44b5532fc9c6.blockers: On-premise architecture blocks direct access to client database and logs; no API calls visible in Rev.io logs indicating requests never reached the system; test card submissions failing with 'input string was not in correct format' error; client requires third-party IT involvement to extract database dumps
cae103a8-b3f0-4cbe-894c-44b5532fc9c6.keyFinding: Payment system integration completely blocked: API calls not reaching Rev.io systems and test card validation failing due to input format errors; on-premise architecture requires client database dump to proceed with troubleshooting.
