323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.title: Quick Start with Ameritel on 2026-04-16
323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.notes:
Notes
Inventory and Purchase Order Management
The team resolved key issues around serial number tracking and purchase order handling to improve inventory accuracy and asset management.
• Resolved product serialization configuration issue ensuring serial number input appears during receiving after adjusting product settings and re-creating the purchase order (01:55)
    ◦ Mickey Kieffer discovered serial numbers weren't prompted during receiving, confirmed product needed "acquire serial number" enabled by Mark Kephart
    ◦ Required deleting and re-adding the product to the purchase order to trigger serial number input correctly
    ◦ This fix ensures accurate tracking of serialized inventory items upon receipt, reducing errors in asset tracking
• Established process for adding Mac addresses via asset conversion after receiving inventory, as custom fields do not exist on products but do on assets (05:00)
    ◦ Mark explained Mac addresses can only be added once inventory is converted into customer assets
    ◦ Mickey and Mark agreed to add Mac addresses and serial numbers when adding assets directly under customers, streamlining device identification for inventory and billing
• Clarified purchase order provider assignment limitations and workaround requiring providers to exist in the system before assignment to POs (07:35)
    ◦ Mickey learned providers cannot be added directly on purchase orders; they must be pre-registered
    ◦ Workaround involves opening inventory in a separate window to add providers, then returning to PO to assign them, preserving entered data
• Defined approach to managing miscellaneous inventory items by creating non-serialized miscellaneous products allowing price adjustments without asset conversion (01:18:35)
    ◦ Mickey wants to track low-cost but important items like cables and straps without complex serialization
    ◦ Mark advised creating miscellaneous product entries with descriptions in product catalog, avoiding asset conversion to keep inventory simpler
    ◦ This approach balances inventory accuracy with operational ease for frequently used small items
Billing, Ticketing, and Invoicing Workflow
The discussion focused on improving invoice accuracy, associating work tickets with billing, and handling complex labor charges to improve billing transparency and tax calculations.
• Established method for adding lump-sum provisioning fees as products or time logs depending on desired billing detail (13:56)
    ◦ Mickey wanted to bill approximately $500 for provisioning despite multiple days worked
    ◦ Mark recommended adding provisioning as a time log with custom hours or as a zero-priced product with price adjustments, depending on need for detailed time tracking
    ◦ This flexibility helps balance customer clarity with internal tracking preferences
• Addressed invoice association and duplication issues from Connectwise data migration requiring manual reversal of duplicate invoices and linking tickets properly (27:04)
    ◦ Mark identified duplicate invoices caused by migration and advised reversing duplicates to maintain clean billing records
    ◦ Highlighted that some invoices lacked ticket associations due to migration timing, impacting visibility of work details on invoices
• Identified current limitation of work performed descriptions not appearing on invoices and the ongoing effort to resolve this by end of the week (30:24)
    ◦ Mark confirmed that invoice templates currently do not pull work performed text, only product descriptions show
    ◦ He is actively working to add this feature soon, which will improve customer understanding and billing accuracy
• Resolved sales tax calculation issues on labor and parts by updating tax codes and confirming Minnesota's tax rules apply to labor (52:46)
    ◦ Mark consulted tax experts and adjusted tax codes to enable proper tax calculation on labor items billed in Minnesota at approximately 9.5%
    ◦ This fix ensures compliance and correct invoicing amounts, preventing revenue leakage or compliance risk
• Implemented invoice status handling and ticket status management recommending setting tickets to "pending" when not ready for billing to keep workflow organized (55:41)
    ◦ Mark advised updating ticket statuses to "pending" via edit ticket to indicate incomplete billing
    ◦ This process helps track progress and avoid premature invoice sending
Asset Management and Customer Inventory Tracking
The team refined asset tracking by integrating serial and Mac address data into customer assets, enhancing traceability post-sale.
• Developed approach to adding assets to customer records prior to billing to capture serial and Mac addresses even before product delivery (59:00)
    ◦ Mark guided Mickey to add assets directly under customer profiles with serial and Mac address details, even if not yet billed or shipped
    ◦ This method allows better inventory visibility and customer service by linking physical devices to customers early
• Confirmed asset type assignment during asset creation is necessary for proper classification and tracking (01:02:07)
    ◦ Mickey entered two assets and selected appropriate asset types to register devices fully
    ◦ Proper asset typing supports reporting and maintenance workflows
• Discussed limitations on storing Mac addresses on purchase orders and preference to keep such data at asset level (01:11:33)
    ◦ Mark recommended removing Mac address fields from purchase orders as it complicates inventory and is better suited for asset records
    ◦ This keeps purchase orders streamlined and asset data centralized for easier updates and searches
Process Improvements and System Usability
Several workflow refinements and system navigation tips were shared to improve operational efficiency and reduce user frustration.
• Clarified navigation shortcuts and system behavior for managing tickets and purchase orders to preserve data entry and maintain links (08:41)
    ◦ Mickey learned to open inventory or provider windows in new tabs to add missing info without losing purchase order data
    ◦ Mark emphasized linking purchase orders to service tickets for better traceability
• Recommended using service tab to access ticket lists directly avoiding multiple clicks through customer accounts (01:15:52)
    ◦ This shortcut saves time when managing multiple tickets and parts entries
• Discussed timesheet entry methods for multiple technicians advising individual time logs per technician for precise tracking (46:38)
    ◦ Mark explained labor for different techs appears as separate line items with dates and hours but not exact times
    ◦ This supports accurate labor cost accounting and billing transparency
• Identified need to fix invoice template formatting for decimal quantities to prevent misrepresentation of labor hours (01:25:43)
    ◦ Mark attempted a template fix but caused errors, plans to continue debugging
    ◦ Accurate decimal display is critical for correct billing and customer trust
Customer and Market Insights
Mickey shared personal insights on customer billing sensitivity, market challenges, and product preferences that inform operational decisions.
• Mickey emphasized the importance of tracking even small inventory items like cables to avoid perceived nickel-and-diming customers (12:05)
    ◦ Small costs add up when used in volume, and proper tracking prevents losses and supports transparent billing
• Discussed personal experience with boating market highlighting storage and feature limitations on new models influencing lifestyle choices (01:45:01)
    ◦ Mickey described challenges finding boats with adequate galley and storage space within budget, favoring older models for livability
    ◦ This anecdote reflects the importance of product fit to customer needs, relevant for product positioning and customer education
• Shared customer sentiment monitoring via system AI which detects negative or positive sentiment in customer notes and emails (01:30:12)
    ◦ Mark explained negative sentiment flags arise from language in tickets or overdue payments, helping prioritize customer outreach
Scheduling and Next Steps
The team agreed on follow-up meetings to finalize migration and transition support, ensuring continuity and issue resolution.
• Scheduled next meetings with client success team for ongoing support and issue resolution on Monday 3–4pm and Thursday 11am–12pm (01:37:25)
    ◦ Mark confirmed client success will take over support after final run-through on Friday pending team availability
    ◦ This formalizes handoff and ensures Mickey’s questions will continue to be addressed by specialized staff
• Planned a graduation call for Friday to complete transition after Josh finishes data migration (01:39:20)
    ◦ This milestone confirms readiness for full operational use and support handoff
• Mark committed to fixing invoice decimal display and monitoring work performed integration progress to improve billing accuracy (01:36:32)
    ◦ He will notify Mickey once work performed text appears on invoices and decimals are correctly formatted
This detailed summary captures all actionable outcomes, system fixes, process adjustments, and planning decisions from the meeting transcript with integrated context and speaker attribution.

323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.title: Quick Start with Ameritel on 2026-04-14
323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.notes:
Notes
Data Migration and API Integration
The team progressed on the data migration but awaited critical API keys to proceed fully.
• API Key Retrieval Enabled Data Migration Start (06:04)
    ◦ Mark Kephart obtained and shared the necessary API keys from the ConnectWise system with the data specialist Josh.
    ◦ Josh had already successfully run the migration on another client, indicating a smooth run expected for Maritel.
    ◦ The process was paused until Maritel’s API keys were accessible; this was resolved during the call to enable continuation.
    ◦ Mark planned to monitor the migration progress closely and update Mickey once complete.
• Need for Public and Private API Keys Identified (15:41)
    ◦ Josh required both public and private API keys for full integration.
    ◦ Mark and Mickey explored ConnectWise to create a dedicated API-only member account with appropriate permissions.
    ◦ The private key visibility is limited to creation time, requiring immediate capture to avoid regeneration delays.
    ◦ This step was critical for the secure, best-practice integration approach.
• Data Migration Status and Concerns (26:35)
    ◦ Josh confirmed hitting all 56 endpoints but had not yet finished the full data pull.
    ◦ Mickey expressed frustration due to pending billing issues and delays from incomplete data access.
    ◦ Mark committed to confirming full migration completion before proceeding with system run-throughs and billing workflows.
    ◦ This data dependency was the main current blocker for further operational progress.
• Integration Limitations with COPS and Alarm.com (29:33)
    ◦ Current integration only syncs customer and contact data from COPS, not billing or charges.
    ◦ Billing remains manual with bulk invoices paid monthly, requiring spreadsheet imports for cost tracking.
    ◦ Mark and Mickey agreed to maintain a running total in the system for annual review and billing rather than automating monthly charges.
    ◦ This approach aligns with Maritel’s preference for manual review before invoicing customers annually.
Billing, Invoicing, and Payment Processing
The discussion clarified billing cycles, invoice formatting, and workflow improvements to avoid past revenue losses.
• Billing Cycle and Invoice Handling Defined (28:10)
    ◦ Maritel bills jobs immediately upon completion, except for annual alarm monitoring billed yearly in advance.
    ◦ Mark set up invoice templates with Maritel’s correct logo and address, removing irrelevant names for professionalism.
    ◦ Mickey requested invoice simplification by grouping labor and parts without line-item technician details on customer-facing invoices.
    ◦ The billing workflow was reviewed, distinguishing between “send to billing” for review and “invoice ticket” for immediate invoicing.
• Invoice Emailing and Revenue Recovery Issues (24:01)
    ◦ Mickey highlighted that previously sent invoices were often not received by customers, causing lost revenue over two months.
    ◦ Mark assessed the system’s email queue and suggested building workflows to trigger invoice reminders and ensure billing consistency.
    ◦ The team agreed to develop workflows to notify Mickey upon quote signing and ticket completion to improve billing follow-up.
    ◦ These process improvements aim to minimize missed invoices and improve cash flow.
• Setup of Late Fees and Tax Policies (34:21)
    ◦ Maritel does not prioritize late fees due to internal billing delays but agreed to a 1.5% late fee after 30 days for future invoices.
    ◦ Returned payment fees were deemed negligible due to their rarity.
    ◦ Mark configured SureTax with appropriate tax codes for labor, serialized, non-serialized, and recurring services, reflecting Minnesota’s comprehensive taxation policy.
    ◦ Mickey confirmed all items and services are taxed uniformly in Minnesota, including labor and shipping.
• Invoice Customization and Labor Tracking (21:55)
    ◦ Labor is invoiced as a lump sum without technician names on invoices but tracked internally via time entry reasons.
    ◦ Mark set up individual time entry reasons for technicians to track labor without cluttering customer invoices.
    ◦ Labor rounding was set to 15-minute intervals with a 60-minute minimum per ticket for accurate billing.
    ◦ This simplifies invoices for customers while maintaining detailed internal accountability.
Billing Profiles and Customer Charge Management
The team developed a manual system for tracking and billing annual monitoring charges with detailed cost allocation.
• Annual Monitoring Charges Managed via Manual Review (33:30)
    ◦ Maritel prefers to keep a running total of monitoring charges updated monthly but bills customers once annually on December 1st.
    ◦ Charges such as base price, testing, open/close events, radio charges, and "other" fees vary by customer and must be individually tracked.
    ◦ Mark proposed setting up recurring services with quantities adjusted to 12 months to track annual costs internally without automatic billing.
    ◦ Mickey emphasized the need to review totals manually before billing to accommodate proration and adjustments.
• Proration and Billing Adjustments for Mid-Year Changes (45:40)
    ◦ If customers add services mid-year, Maritel manually calculates prorated charges plus next year’s fees.
    ◦ Mark confirmed that tickets can be kept open to accumulate charges until December invoicing, supporting Maritel’s billing approach.
    ◦ The system allows adding sub-charges for detailed cost tracking (e.g., COPS base rate, testing, open/close, radio, other).
    ◦ This flexibility accommodates complex billing scenarios and ensures profit margin visibility.
• Data Import and Synchronization Limitations (48:08)
    ◦ Integration with COPS does not currently pull billing charges into the system; it only syncs customer data one-way from Revio to COPS.
    ◦ Mickey decided not to push all customers to COPS due to selective monitoring needs.
    ◦ Mark noted the ability to control which customers sync to COPS and suggested involving Brenda in future calls for deeper training.
    ◦ This selective synchronization supports Maritel’s operational preferences.
• Job Costing Visibility via Tickets (51:32)
    ◦ Mark confirmed that ticket-level tracking provides cost versus profit analysis by accumulating labor and parts for alarm monitoring jobs.
    ◦ Tickets remain open through the year for charge accumulation and are invoiced at year-end, matching Maritel’s manual billing strategy.
    ◦ Line items can be hidden from customer invoices while retaining detailed internal records.
    ◦ This approach balances billing transparency with operational tracking needs.
Asset and Warranty Management
The team addressed detailed tracking needs for purchased equipment, warranties, and asset management.
• Warranty and Asset Tracking Fields Added (54:40)
    ◦ Mark created a warranty expiration date field in purchase orders to document product warranty periods.
    ◦ Mickey emphasized the importance of tracking serial numbers, MAC addresses, passwords, and warranty details at receipt to avoid service issues.
    ◦ Custom fields for warranties and asset details will be added both to purchase orders and assets for comprehensive coverage.
    ◦ This allows quick identification of assets, their origin, and warranty status for maintenance decisions.
• Product vs. Asset Field Limitations Clarified (01:00:58)
    ◦ Custom fields are limited to assets, not products, so detailed tracking applies after items leave inventory.
    ◦ Mickey requested that all unique identifiers travel with the asset record to facilitate searching by serial number and license tracking.
    ◦ Mark confirmed items can be searched by serial number but suggested entering detailed info on assets post-sale.
    ◦ This ensures accurate inventory and service management.
• Telephony Equipment Password Tracking Highlighted (56:55)
    ◦ Mickey explained the need to store phone-specific passwords for Grandstream and Yealink phones to enable remote management.
    ◦ Mark suggested adding checkboxes or custom fields on assets for management tool registration status.
    ◦ Both agreed to ensure purchase orders and assets reflect these critical details to avoid service disruptions.
    ◦ This supports proactive device management and security.
Workflow Automation and Training
Workflows will be built to improve billing accuracy and notification, while training plans for mobile app usage were discussed.
• Workflow Creation for Billing and Notifications Planned (27:14)
    ◦ Mark and Mickey agreed to build workflows triggering reminders for billing after ticket completion and quote signing.
    ◦ Notifications will initially be sent only to Mickey, with potential expansion to other recipients later.
    ◦ These workflows aim to close the gap on missed invoices and improve billing timeliness.
    ◦ Mark scheduled follow-ups to finalize these workflows aligned with the full system run-through.
• Mobile App Adoption Considered (27:40)
    ◦ Mark demonstrated the mobile app’s signature capture feature used by field technicians.
    ◦ Mickey indicated only Ben may be trained to use the mobile app, with others not expected to adopt it currently.
    ◦ Mark offered to train Ben in the next call or let Mickey handle it as preferred.
    ◦ This balances operational efficiency with team readiness.
• Next Training and System Run-Through Scheduled (59:43)
    ◦ The next session was set for the following day at 3:00 PM, allowing time to complete data migration by 11:00 AM.
    ◦ Mark planned to finalize tax setups, invoice templates, and workflow builds before the session.
    ◦ The goal is to conduct a comprehensive end-to-end system demonstration with all components integrated.
    ◦ This timeline reflects a focus on readiness before full operational rollout.
Document and Signature Management
Templates for customer authorization and payment forms were created to streamline compliance and order processing.
• Letter of Agency (LOA) Forms Digitized and Integrated (37:41)
    ◦ Mark imported LOA local and toll-free templates as fillable documents with signature blocks.
    ◦ Mickey will pre-fill most fields before uploading; customers complete signatures and essential identifiers.
    ◦ The ability to edit within the system was discussed but decided on uploading pre-filled PDFs for simplicity.
    ◦ This streamlines regulatory compliance and customer authorization workflows.
• Payment Authorization Forms Combined (47:58)
    ◦ Credit card and ACH authorization forms were merged into a single payment form for each LOA template.
    ◦ Mark configured required fields and radio buttons to ensure proper payment method selection.
    ◦ Mickey confirmed customers can fill either form, with flexibility for future payment options.
    ◦ This consolidation reduces administrative overhead and improves customer experience.
• Form Management and Signature Workflow Finalized (58:45)
    ◦ Mark set up two final templates: local LOA with finance info and toll-free LOA with finance info.
    ◦ Customers receive these separately but can print and distribute pages individually as needed.
    ◦ The system supports sending one form at a time or combining into a single document for signature routing.
    ◦ This supports Maritel’s complex order processing and document management needs.

323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.title: Quick Start with Ameritel on 2026-04-14
323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.notes:
Notes
Inventory and Purchase Order Management
The team established a detailed inventory system with serial number tracking and purchase order workflows to improve asset visibility and billing accuracy.
• Inventory Setup with Serial Tracking Implemented marked by Mark Kephart, they enabled serial number tracking for products to know exactly which customer owns which asset and where items are physically located (02:43)
    ◦ Items sold automatically convert to customer assets for clear ownership records.
    ◦ Serial numbers can be scanned via barcode scanners to speed up receiving and shipping.
    ◦ Non-serialized items can also be tracked in inventory without serial numbers.
• Purchase Order Process Defined with Vendor Details including adding vendors like APD Alarm Product Distributors with contact info, website, and login reference fields for quick access when ordering (10:15)
    ◦ POs can be created linked to tickets or independently as blanket orders for vendor management.
    ◦ The system auto-generates PO numbers starting at 1, with a request to customize numbering to include year prefixes for better tracking.
• Receiving and Shipping Workflows Established requiring serial numbers upon receiving serialized items and allowing import of serial lists for efficiency (25:17)
    ◦ Shipping requires reserving inventory serials to prevent selling unavailable stock.
    ◦ Transfers between warehouse and vehicles are tracked with reasons for proper inventory allocation.
• Feature Requests to Improve PO Numbering and Branding included adding company logo and phone number to POs and customizing starting PO numbers to reflect fiscal years, enabling easy aging of inventory and invoices (19:15)
    ◦ Current system limitations prevent changing starting invoice numbers, but requests submitted to support better business tracking.
• Business Impact expected to reduce errors in billing, improve asset management, and streamline vendor interactions through centralization and automation of inventory and purchase order data.
Billing, Invoicing, and Payment Processing
The discussion clarified billing flows with automation for invoicing, payment recording, and credit management, emphasizing flexibility for discounts and various payment types.
• Billing Workflow with Ticket Integration allows tickets to generate billing charges including parts, labor, and shipping, with options to review before invoicing or invoice directly (33:37)
    ◦ Invoices include lump sum tax calculations based on customer location and product tax codes.
    ◦ Invoices can be emailed or downloaded for customer delivery.
• Payment Recording Supports Multiple Methods including checks, ACH, credit cards (processed externally), and cash, with possible dropdowns for payment types and manual recording of discounts as credit line items (38:52)
    ◦ Courtesy discounts can be recorded as taxable credits to adjust invoices correctly.
• Invoice Template Customization in Progress to include clickable payment links and QR codes for customer convenience and company branding (52:45)
    ◦ Logo placement issues identified and being resolved to improve professional appearance on invoices.
• Late Fee Policy Configured with 1.5% interest after 30 days plus a minimum late fee of $10, balancing customer leniency and business cash flow protection (01:02:21)
• Business Impact improves cash flow through timely invoicing and payment capture, while providing flexibility for customer-specific billing and discounts.
Recurring Services and Contract Billing Strategy
A major focus was on managing annual alarm monitoring contracts with prepaid billing and internal cost allocation to inform pricing strategy and customer billing.
• Recurring Services Set Up for Annual Alarm Monitoring simplifies billing customers yearly while tracking cost sources from multiple providers (01:08:36)
    ◦ Providers like Telguard, Alarm.com, and COPS are used simultaneously for different service components.
• Cost Allocation and Contract Management Discussed to bill customers a flat annual rate upfront while internally tracking monthly provider charges against each customer (01:14:21)
    ◦ Charges from providers are recorded as internal billings but not invoiced to customers, preserving profit margins and competitive pricing.
    ◦ End-of-year reviews can compare prepaid revenue versus actual costs to adjust future pricing and discounts.
• Integration Opportunities with Providers noted, with plans to involve integration lead Brenda to explore automated billing data imports from providers such as COPS or Alarm.com (01:14:46)
• Flexibility to Log Time Against Contracts allows capturing labor costs under contracts for comprehensive cost tracking (01:22:12)
• Business Impact enables accurate profit analysis on prepaid contracts and helps maintain competitive customer pricing while managing operational costs precisely.
Product and Labor Management
The team refined product catalog use, labor tracking, and subcontractor billing to simplify invoicing and cost management.
• Product Catalog Used for Labor Types and Services with labor types defined for time entry reasons linked to tickets and invoices (01:50)
    ◦ Installation labor and phone support labor categories created for clarity.
• Subcontractor Billing Modeled as Products to input subcontractor costs as expenses and bill at marked-up rates without requiring them to log into the system (02:00:54)
    ◦ Labor products created for individual subcontractors with cost and bill rates separately defined (e.g., Ben Otto at $65 cost, billed at $100) (02:01:23)
    ◦ Labor entries can be grouped under generic service or labor product names to avoid multiple line items on customer invoices, simplifying presentation (02:10:42)
• Invoice Presentation Adjusted to Avoid Multiple Labor Lines to prevent confusing customers about rate differences between subcontractors (02:12:08)
    ◦ Work ongoing to bundle labor entries into single line items on invoices.
• Business Impact streamlines labor cost tracking and billing while maintaining clear margins and reducing invoice complexity.
Scheduling, Task, and Project Management
The meeting covered task scheduling, calendar integration, and project tracking, focused on improving personal organization and limited team coordination.
• Scheduling and Dispatch Board Usage allows creating tasks linked to tickets with reminders and calendar entries, managed mainly by a small team of three (01:23:05)
    ◦ Tasks can be assigned to individuals with start dates and linked contacts to keep work organized.
• Outlook Calendar Integration Completed syncing a dedicated Rev IO calendar with Outlook to separate work events from personal or other calendars, keeping visibility focused (01:24:50)
    ◦ Sync is bidirectional but may take time to fully reflect changes; personal calendar events excluded by using a dedicated calendar.
• Project Management Features Introduced including project creation, phases, milestones, and ticket linking to track progress and workload (01:47:29)
    ◦ Example project created to clean and reorganize warehouse inventory with estimated hours and task assignments.
    ◦ Activity feeds and in-app notifications support team communication and progress tracking.
• Mobile App Deployment Initiated with instructions to download Rev IO app and connect using company URL for on-the-go access (01:52:10)
• Business Impact improves task visibility, calendar organization, and project tracking for better workflow and personal productivity.
User Experience and Feature Requests
Several usability improvements and feature requests were identified to enhance operational efficiency and user satisfaction.
• Enhancements Needed for Contact Management include adding and editing contacts directly within tickets or emails to avoid workflow interruptions (46:19)
    ◦ Requested ability to add contacts on the fly and include multiple phones like cell numbers for screen pop integration.
• Invoice and Purchase Order Template Improvements to add company logos, phone numbers, and customizable numbering schemes for better branding and easier document tracking (19:15)
• Automatic BCC for Billing Emails Requested to ensure internal copies of invoices are always received without manual steps (49:37)
• Notes on Calendar Events feature currently missing; users cannot add personal notes to shared calendar events without affecting others (02:17:22)
• Login and Authentication Issues Addressed with guidance on correct username format and two-factor authentication setup (01:57:19)
• Business Impact these improvements aim to reduce manual work, increase data accuracy, and improve communication both internally and with customers.

323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.title: Meeting with Ameritel on 2026-03-31
323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.notes: Mickey met for a brief time and then had to go take care of a customer issue. We are in the process of resceduling.

323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.title: Quick Start with Ameritel on 2026-03-30
323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.notes:
Notes
ConnectWise Data Reporting and Backup
The team focused on generating detailed service ticket reports and exploring options for full data backup to secure historical access.
• Creation of Service Notes Report Using Report Writer (00:04)
    ◦ Mark and Mickey navigated the Report Writer tool to extract service ticket notes joined by ticket ID.
    ◦ They selected key fields such as contact rec ID, created by, UTC timestamps, and notes to build a comprehensive report.
    ◦ The report was successfully previewed, exported as a CSV file named "Service Notes," and saved as a custom report for future use.
    ◦ This report covers all service notes and aligns with Mickey’s existing Excel spreadsheet tabs for customers, contacts, products, tickets, and service notes.
• Challenges and Strategy for Full Data Backup (00:19)
    ◦ ConnectWise does not offer a simple self-service full database backup; it requires opening a ticket and incurs a charge of approximately $300/hour with a 10-day turnaround.
    ◦ Mark recommended using AI tools like Glean AI to explore alternative backup methods without ConnectWise involvement.
    ◦ Mickey emphasized the need for accessible backups to handle audits and historical data retrieval after losing direct ConnectWise access.
    ◦ No internal expertise on ConnectWise full backups exists, so reliance on external AI or vendor support is necessary.
Vendor and Product Data Management
The discussion centered on importing vendor lists, categorizing products, and managing purchase orders to streamline procurement and inventory tracking.
• Vendor List Export and Import Strategy (00:30)
    ◦ Mickey has approximately 200 vendors in ConnectWise, too many for manual entry, so exporting the full vendor list was preferred.
    ◦ Key primary vendors include Teledynamics and Jenny, which will be ported over to the new system.
    ◦ Mark advised sending vendor exports as attachments for import into PSA, streamlining the transition.
    ◦ The team agreed to review and compare vendor data post-import for accuracy and completeness.
• Product Categorization and Purchase Order Setup (00:28)
    ◦ System tables allow adding product categories and extra fields to improve product search and classification in PSA.
    ◦ Mickey plans to create categories like "Viya" and "IP Office" for better product organization.
    ◦ Purchase orders are used regularly, with serial numbers and distributor names tracked, avoiding packing lists to reduce confusion.
    ◦ Mark highlighted the ability to add custom fields on purchase orders to capture subcomponent details like SD card numbers and license info.
Billing and Contract Configuration
The team worked through billing methods, recurring service setup, and contract management to support complex client billing needs.
• Annual and Monthly Billing Setup for Alarm Monitoring (00:42)
    ◦ Mickey confirmed alarm monitoring billing occurs annually in December, while most other charges are one-time.
    ◦ PSA requires setting up recurring services under contracts to handle annual billing properly.
    ◦ Pricing varies per customer based on service features like video or key cards, necessitating flexible pricing on recurring services.
    ◦ Mark and Mickey discussed assigning providers to recurring services, with the ability to adjust pricing dynamically per customer.
• Handling Multiple Providers and Service Components (00:52)
    ◦ Customers often have multiple providers for alarm monitoring and connectivity, like Alarm.com, Cops, and Telguard.
    ◦ Mickey requires tracking multiple vendors per customer for billing and purchasing, despite only one provider field in PSA.
    ◦ Mark proposed using custom fields and multiple product records to represent separate providers and features.
    ◦ Additional monthly fees for integrations and features are bundled into a single customer invoice annually but tracked individually internally.
Customer and Asset Management Enhancements
The team configured custom fields for carriers, account credentials, and detailed asset tracking to improve customer service and operational accuracy.
• Custom Fields for Carrier Accounts and Credentials (01:05)
    ◦ Mickey needs to store multiple carrier accounts per customer, including account numbers, PINs, dealer IDs, and subaccount names.
    ◦ Mark added fields like "Intermediate subaccount" and aligned layout for easy data entry and retrieval.
    ◦ These fields apply retroactively to existing customers, allowing filling in missing data for legacy accounts.
    ◦ This setup supports quick access to carrier login info when opening tickets or resolving issues.
• Asset Tracking for Serial Numbers, MAC Addresses, and Passwords (01:36)
    ◦ Assets now include fields for serial numbers, MAC addresses, and device passwords (e.g., for Grandstream phones).
    ◦ Passwords are critical for locked devices and admin access, ensuring service continuity.
    ◦ Mickey emphasized the need to associate licenses with SD cards and track nested components for products like Avaya IP Office.
    ◦ Searching assets by serial number is supported; MAC address search is not due to custom field limitations.
Quote and Sales Process Workflow
The team reviewed PSA quoting capabilities, document signing, and integration with sales workflows to improve customer engagement and billing accuracy.
• Quote Creation and Template Customization (01:10)
    ◦ PSA allows creating editable quotes with drag-and-drop blocks and product catalogs for line items.
    ◦ Mickey can view SKU, vendor, cost, margin, and notes within quotes to assess profitability before sending.
    ◦ Multiple sections can separate one-time fees from recurring charges for clarity.
    ◦ Quotes generate PDFs sent via PandaDoc, enabling electronic signatures and automated status tracking.
• Handling Multiple Customer Forms and Signatures (01:20)
    ◦ Mickey manages various customer forms like Letters of Agency (LOA), credit card, ACH authorization, and SMS text messaging consents.
    ◦ These forms are unique per customer and currently created in Excel or Adobe, with manual signature fields.
    ◦ Mark advised uploading PDFs to PandaDoc without signature fields and adding signature blocks directly in the platform.
    ◦ This approach streamlines sending multiple documents for e-signature while keeping customer-specific data secure.
Time Tracking and Labor Management
The discussion addressed capturing billable and non-billable time effectively to improve revenue recognition and operational insight.
• Time Entry Linked to Tickets or Customers (01:43)
    ◦ PSA requires a ticket to start a timer; otherwise, users must create blank tickets to capture time spent.
    ◦ Mickey wants to track non-revenue producing (NRPB) activities like information calls and research without immediate ticket creation.
    ◦ Mark suggested a catch-all ticket for logging such time and associating it later with specific customers or tickets.
    ◦ Time entry reasons can be customized (e.g., "Labor Research") to categorize work and support later billing decisions.
• Challenges with Retrospective Time Association (01:44)
    ◦ Time logged must be assigned to an existing ticket; deleting tickets does not reassign time elsewhere.
    ◦ Mickey highlighted the need to track discretionary service calls and partial billings reflecting actual time spent.
    ◦ Mark acknowledged the need for flexible workflows capturing all time, billing or not, to maximize revenue capture.
    ◦ They planned to revisit and refine time tracking processes in the next meeting.
Next Steps and Scheduling
The meeting concluded with action plans for data migration, system configuration, and future sessions to finalize PSA setup.
• Data Migration and Backup Ticket Submission (01:55)
    ◦ Mark will submit a data migration request to import Mickey’s service notes and vendor lists, targeting completion by Friday.
    ◦ He will continue investigating ways to obtain full database backups accessible without ConnectWise support.
    ◦ Mickey emphasized the importance of backup access for tax audits and historical reporting.
• Follow-up Meeting Scheduled (01:51)
    ◦ They agreed to meet the following Tuesday from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM Central Time to continue PSA configuration.
    ◦ Calendar timezone issues were resolved during the call to ensure correct scheduling across zones.
    ◦ Mark confirmed sending the meeting invite and using Teams for the session.
• Ongoing System Configuration and Training (01:57)
    ◦ Mickey will keep progressing through PSA tutorials and admin checklists to finalize company settings.
    ◦ Mark offered support on troubleshooting tutorial navigation issues and will follow up as needed.
    ◦ Both committed to iterative sessions to tailor PSA processes to Mickey’s business needs.

323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.title: Quick Start with Ameritel on 2026-03-24
323a59b7-e7b2-81df-a7e0-ca56aefdee92.notes:
Notes
Email System Configuration
The team focused on setting up outgoing email settings and DNS record management to enable smooth ticketing email workflows.
• Outgoing Email Setup Using Support Address (02:01)
    ◦ Configured system emails to come from support@ameritelink.net labeled “Ameritel Support.”
    ◦ This email handles ticket communication but excludes invoices, which use a separate AR address.
    ◦ Mark explained the distinction between incoming ticket creation emails and outgoing customer notifications.
    ◦ Expected outcome is consistent customer-facing email branding for support tickets.
• DNS and Domain Verification Challenges with Mailgun (03:14)
    ◦ Mickey logged into Mailgun to add DNS records required for email authentication.
    ◦ Mailgun acts as an email service but does not host DNS, so DNS changes must be made at the domain registrar.
    ◦ Their domain registrar is GoDaddy, but DNS management access was restricted due to DNS Made Easy handling their DNS.
    ◦ Action assigned to Mickey’s team member Brett to add necessary DNS records via DNS Made Easy to enable SendGrid email validation.
    ◦ Validation expected to take 15 minutes to 48 hours, after which outgoing email functions will be confirmed.
• Forwarding SendGrid Confirmation Email for DNS Setup (03:25)
    ◦ Mark sent the SendGrid DNS setup email to Mickey to forward to Brett for action.
    ◦ Emphasized relying on Brett’s expertise to place records correctly since Mailgun cannot add DNS entries directly.
    ◦ Follow-up planned after Brett’s action to complete email system setup.
PSA System Configuration and Ticketing Setup
They configured system settings in the PSA platform to streamline asset management, ticket categorization, and time tracking.
• System Tables and Asset Categorization Customization (31:00)
    ◦ Mark showed how to add and edit asset types for tracking customer equipment and licenses.
    ◦ Vendor types can include subcontractors, allowing flexible categorization of service providers.
    ◦ Shipping methods are customizable to fit operational needs.
    ◦ This setup supports better product catalog management and asset tracking tied to customers.
• Ticket Status and Priority Color Coding (33:02)
    ◦ Configured five ticket statuses: New (green), Open (blue), Pending (yellow), Closed (red), and Void (gray).
    ◦ Closed means ticket completion and billing done; tickets can be reopened if needed.
    ◦ Priority levels assigned colors: High (bright red), Medium (yellow), and Low (blue/green).
    ◦ These visual cues aim to improve ticket triage and workflow efficiency.
• Time Tracking and Billing Categories (37:00)
    ◦ Created billable time entry reasons like labor phone support with a 15-minute rounding minimum to balance billing precision and customer goodwill.
    ◦ Hourly rates set at $100 for normal hours, $150 after hours, and $200 on holidays, with options to track cost per technician.
    ◦ Non-billable categories like labor research added to track unbillable work without billing customers.
    ◦ Multiple timers can run simultaneously for accurate time capture across tasks.
• Ticket Creation Workflow Emphasized (06:38)
    ◦ Recommended creating a ticket for every customer call to document work and facilitate billing.
    ◦ Tickets must be associated with a customer before starting the timer to track labor accurately.
    ◦ The system supports macros to auto-fill repetitive ticket data, though none set up yet.
    ◦ Mickey plans to adopt this disciplined ticketing and timing approach for better service tracking.
Customer and Contact Management
The meeting detailed how to organize customer data, contacts, notes, and integrate external system links for easier remote support.
• Custom Fields for Remote Access and Carrier Links (48:11)
    ◦ Added custom fields for remote access tools like Screen Connect and Take Control as URLs attached to each customer.
    ◦ Additional carrier login links such as Intermedia, Alarm.com, Carrier Cops, and Secure were added for quick account access.
    ◦ Links are not clickable hotlinks yet; user must copy and paste into browsers.
    ◦ Mark plans to request a feature for clickable links in future system updates.
• Customer Tags for Better Segmentation (22:38)
    ◦ System tags created for common attributes like Intermediate, Avaya, or IP Office customers.
    ◦ Multiple tags can be applied per customer to reflect their product or service profile.
    ◦ Tags improve searchability and help classify customers for reporting or operational purposes.
• Notes and Hot Notes for Critical Info (42:43)
    ◦ Introduced pop-up notes that appear when opening a customer record for urgent info like access codes or passwords.
    ◦ Notes are permission-based; some users can dismiss globally, others only for themselves.
    ◦ This feature helps field techs quickly see important customer info without searching.
• Contact Roles and Billing Contacts (27:37)
    ◦ Customers can have multiple contacts with customizable contact types such as billing or primary contact.
    ◦ Only one primary contact is allowed, but additional contacts can be marked inactive as needed.
    ◦ Mickey requested AR reports to include contact emails and phone numbers for direct follow-up on overdue accounts; Mark agreed to investigate feasibility.
Purchase Orders, Inventory, and Asset Management
The team reviewed purchase order numbering, inventory tracking, and asset assignment linked to customers.
• Purchase Order Numbering Preferences (53:22)
    ◦ Mickey prefers starting PO numbers with the year prefix (e.g., 26-0001) for easy aging recognition.
    ◦ Current system auto-increments from 1; Mark to check if starting PO numbers at a higher base like 10,000 is possible.
    ◦ Custom fields can store manual PO numbers to align with Mickey’s preferred format.
• Vendor Data Import and Historical Data Archiving (56:10)
    ◦ Discussed exporting vendor and customer data from ConnectWise for import into Tiger Paw replacement.
    ◦ Mark will seek internal experts to help with ConnectWise data export and archiving for historical reference.
    ◦ Goal is to preserve 4+ years of historical data for lookup after switching systems.
• Inventory and Asset Serial Number Tracking (55:00)
    ◦ Assets assigned to customers include serial numbers and installation dates for tracking equipment lifecycle.
    ◦ Inventory serial numbers can be matched to POs and reserved for shipment to customers.
    ◦ Mickey wants visibility on serial numbers of received items linked back to POs; Mark confirmed this linkage is supported.
• On-the-Fly Purchase Order Creation (52:00)
    ◦ Mickey emphasized need to generate POs quickly during vendor interactions for accurate purchasing records.
    ◦ System auto-generates PO numbers and supports CSV import of serial numbers from providers to update inventory.
    ◦ This improves purchasing accuracy and inventory reconciliation compared to previous systems.
Billing and Payment Processing
They reviewed billing profiles, payment methods, and customer portal capabilities relevant to invoicing and collections.
• Annual Contract Billing Outside Billing Profiles (11:23)
    ◦ Annual alarm and IP office support contracts billed per customer contract rather than billing profiles.
    ◦ Mickey bills jobs immediately upon completion, with payment due on receipt.
    ◦ Parent-child account relationships enable consolidated invoicing to a parent company when appropriate.
• Customer Portal and Payment Methods (30:24)
    ◦ Customers can access a portal to open tickets, view invoices, and make payments.
    ◦ Mickey’s company uses a QR code on invoices directing customers to a separate payment page outside PSA’s portal.
    ◦ Mark will modify invoice templates to replace PSA portal links with Mickey’s payment portal QR code for smoother payment experience.
• API Integration for Auto-Populated Payments (35:00)
    ◦ Mickey shared API keys and QR code for their payment system, exploring auto-filling invoice numbers and amounts during payment.
    ◦ Mark expressed uncertainty about feasibility and will consult further but confirmed having API info is essential for evaluation.
    ◦ This integration could streamline payment matching and reduce manual reconciliation.
• Manual Payment Entry and ACH Considerations (01:02)
    ◦ Payments by check or ACH require manual entry and marking invoices as paid.
    ◦ ACH fees are low; current volume (about 20 charges/year) does not justify switching payment processors now.
    ◦ Billing configuration to be revisited when recurring payments are discussed.
Calendar and Scheduling Integration
They planned calendar synchronization and resource scheduling to improve appointment booking and visibility.
• Calendar Item Creation and Sync (51:44)
    ◦ Calendar items can be added per customer for appointments like telephone number porting.
    ◦ Items support custom naming and time duration.
    ◦ Integration with user calendars (e.g., Outlook) planned to provide unified schedule views.
• Tech Resource Calendar Sharing (53:18)
    ◦ Discussion about syncing technician Ben’s calendar to PSA to view availability for booking.
    ◦ Supported calendar types include Outlook and Google Calendar.
    ◦ Mark noted some calendar types may not be compatible; confirmation needed.
• Time Zone Correction (59:10)
    ◦ Time zone was initially set incorrectly to Eastern; changed to Central (Chicago) to match Mickey’s location.
    ◦ Accurate time zone setting is important for appointment and ticket scheduling accuracy.
Next Steps and Follow-Up
The meeting closed with scheduling and data migration updates.
• Tiger Paw Data Upload Timing (59:30)
    ◦ Data engineering team working on importing Tiger Paw data, targeting completion by next Friday or sooner.
    ◦ Mickey wants to start testing and using the system before his upcoming planned downtime.
    ◦ Mark will urge the team to accelerate delivery for earlier access.
• Follow-Up Meeting Rescheduled (02:00)
    ◦ Next session moved from Tuesday 9 AM to Wednesday 3 PM Central to accommodate Mickey’s schedule.
    ◦ Meeting will focus on PSA configuration continuation with possible inclusion of Mark’s manager.
• Action on DNS Records and Payment API (02:02)
    ◦ Mickey’s team will work with vendor Brett on DNS records for email setup.
    ◦ Mark to follow up with Mickey if further payment API details or credentials are needed.
    ◦ Both agreed to keep communication open for smooth progress.

317a59b7-e7b2-8052-85bc-d6036b9c4ce8.notes:
Notes
Quote and Inventory Integration
The team clarified the current independence and integration limits between quoting, inventory, and billing systems to streamline workflows.
• Quotes and Taxes Are Separate from Billing with Panda Docs Integration (00:01)
    ◦ Quotes are created with Panda Docs and do not automatically sync with billing requests.
    ◦ Tax calculation on quotes currently fails with a 400 error due to differing tax systems; billing uses CSI instead of SureTax.
    ◦ Quotes must be manually downloaded and attached to billing customers to maintain records since data does not propagate automatically.
    ◦ Mark Kephart created a ticket to address the tax calculation issue for resolution.
• Inventory Management Begins Post-Quote Signing (00:14)
    ◦ Inventory processes activate only after a quote is signed and completed, generating a service ticket that manages inventory allocation.
    ◦ Serialized inventory items require manual reservation before invoicing; the system checks stock availability at this stage.
    ◦ Both warehouse stocking and drop shipping are supported; users must manually select serial numbers from specific locations before invoicing.
    ◦ Inventory and billing sides operate independently; inventory data does not flow into billing, causing potential double effort in tracking serial numbers.
• Recurring Services Require Manual Activation and Categorization (00:20)
    ◦ Recurring services do not auto-activate upon quote signing; users must manually activate contracts to trigger billing.
    ◦ Services are mostly categorized under "Voice Services," simplifying manual classification but still requiring oversight.
    ◦ The system lacks automation in assigning service types and rate decks at contract activation, increasing manual workload.
    ◦ Kevin Schramm emphasized that PSA should eventually centralize customer interactions, relegating billing to purely financial transactions.
• Strategic Approach to PSA and Billing Integration (00:31)
    ◦ The ultimate goal is a single platform integrating PSA functionalities into billing for a seamless user experience.
    ◦ Currently, customers face duplicate systems for quoting, inventory, and ticketing, causing confusion and inefficiency.
    ◦ Kevin recommended phased integration, starting with replacing inventory and ticketing modules in billing with PSA's superior functionality.
    ◦ This consolidation would simplify maintenance and reduce redundancies, while enabling easier upsell of PSA features.
Inventory System and Process Improvements
The inventory module offers strong base functionality with room for usability and process enhancements to reduce operational friction.
• Inventory Matrix Enables Detailed Stock and Location Transfers (00:49)
    ◦ Users can bulk manage inventory items, transfer serial numbered items between locations, and track reservations.
    ◦ Transfers require mandatory notes to document reasons, enforcing accountability and audit trails.
    ◦ Barcode scanning integration supports efficient serial number entry for large shipments, easing initial data input.
    ◦ Mark Liescke requested adding product images to the inventory overview to help technicians identify correct items easily.
• Current Limitations and User Feedback on Inventory Handling (00:52)
    ◦ The system remembers the last used location during item addition, which can cause errors if users forget to change it.
    ◦ Bulk serial number uploading is possible via CSV import, but manual entry remains tedious for large volumes initially.
    ◦ Users flagged a desire for better UI features like overlapping UI elements (e.g., orange bar overlapping grey) to match existing quote templates.
    ◦ The inventory and billing systems remain siloed, requiring additional manual coordination for order fulfillment tracking.
Billing and Invoice Workflow
Billing remains a distinct system with manual handoffs from PSA, but new reporting and automation features improve operational control.
• Invoice Creation and Management Workflow (00:27)
    ◦ Inventory-reserved items from PSA quotes can be sent to billing for invoice review or immediate invoicing.
    ◦ Billing users can generate invoices in bulk, email customers, and reverse charges if needed.
    ◦ Invoice templates currently show overall customer balance, which may confuse customers about specific charges.
    ◦ Kevin favored Panda Docs for quote customization but expressed concern about complexity during ongoing integration work.
• Payment Processing and Collection Tools (00:57)
    ◦ Payment processing is integrated via Trx (formerly Paya), with some ongoing confusion due to vendor name changes and communication lapses.
    ◦ Billing includes a collections queue for manual intervention; automated emails are supported but no automated calling is planned.
    ◦ Permissions allow hiding billing modules from users who do not require access, simplifying user experience.
    ◦ Reporting features on the billing side are used heavily and will be migrated to PSA with anticipated downtime during migration.
• Advanced Reporting and Analytics in PSA (01:04)
    ◦ PSA provides real-time dashboards with ticket profitability, customer aging, and cross-table SQL query support.
    ◦ Users can build, customize, and schedule reports, gaining enhanced flexibility over previous systems.
    ◦ Kevin values the ability to create cross-table queries independently, reducing reliance on support for complex reports.
    ◦ Pre-built datasets and reports will be available upon migration, with full SQL access to customize analytics.
Project Management and Scheduling Enhancements
PSA supports project and scheduling management with flexible phases, milestones, and calendar integrations to improve field operations.
• Project Module Supports Multi-Phase Tracking and Notifications (01:11)
    ◦ Projects can be created with phases and milestones, assigned roles, and include linked tickets for granular tracking.
    ◦ An activity feed consolidates updates across tickets and project status, aiding transparency and coordination.
    ◦ In-app notifications and emails can be sent to team members for urgent updates, improving responsiveness.
    ◦ Future billing for entire projects is planned but flagged as potentially complex to implement.
• Scheduling Integrates with Outlook Calendars for User Sync (01:31)
    ◦ PSA can sync user calendars bidirectionally with Outlook, refreshing every 10 minutes by default.
    ◦ Each user must individually connect their calendar; integration appears stable without known expiration.
    ◦ Currently, all event details are visible to viewers; no permission-based event hiding exists yet.
    ◦ Users suggested separating personal and work calendars in Outlook to control visibility due to privacy concerns.
• Dispatch Board Facilitates Task Assignment but Lacks Primary Tech Auto-Assignment (01:28)
    ◦ Tickets can be assigned tasks and dragged onto calendars or dispatch boards for scheduling.
    ◦ Assigned technicians do not automatically become primary techs on tickets, requiring manual updates.
    ◦ Kevin recommended a settings option to enable automatic linking of assigned techs, saving time.
    ◦ The system shows overlapping tasks visually, helping avoid double booking.
Workflow Automation and User Notifications
PSA supports customizable workflows to automate notifications and task triggers, enhancing communication and operational efficiency.
• Workflow Setup for Quote Sign Notifications (01:39)
    ◦ Workflows trigger on ticket creation filtered by description containing "quote signed" to narrow notifications.
    ◦ Notifications can be sent via email, in-app, or SMS, with separate workflows required per notification type.
    ◦ Both Kevin and Mark opted to receive email notifications on quote sign events to stay informed.
    ◦ Use of tokens in emails personalizes messages with customer and creator info, enhancing clarity.
• Multiple Reaction Types Require Multiple Workflows (01:42)
    ◦ PSA does not currently support multiple reaction types in one workflow, necessitating duplicates for each channel.
    ◦ Teams are advised to balance notification volume to avoid spamming users with redundant alerts.
    ◦ Workflow management is accessed via admin settings, allowing configuration of triggers and recipient lists.
Next Steps and Training Plans
The team agreed on immediate follow-ups and training schedules to onboard field staff and improve PSA adoption.
• Upcoming Training for Field Techs on Ticketing and Time Logging (02:14)
    ◦ Mark Kephart requested emails of field techs to invite them for a focused training session.
    ◦ The training will cover ticket handling, time logging, and inventory management to ensure smooth adoption.
    ◦ Sessions will be limited to 1.5 hours going forward to avoid fatigue and maintain engagement.
• Scheduling Next Meeting and Timeline for PSA Activation (02:16)
    ◦ Next session tentatively set for April 15th, allowing the team two weeks to prepare amid busy schedules.
    ◦ Goal remains to have PSA fully activated by the end of April, with flexibility to adjust as needed.
    ◦ Mark Liescke indicated intent to input a new opportunity into PSA soon, testing communication between quoting and opportunity modules.
• Ongoing Feedback and Feature Requests (02:02)
    ◦ Mark Liescke highlighted the need for a "provider customer account number" field in PSA to map PBX billing correctly.
    ◦ Kevin explained PBX uses group and department mappings in daily CDR uploads to associate calls with customers.
    ◦ Mark Kephart proposed adding this as a custom field in PSA customers or recurring services to align with billing workflows.
    ◦ The team acknowledged some manual steps remain, with plans to automate as system maturity increases.

22836d05-4b01-4dd3-8fef-b4cdd7a949ac.title: Meeting with Georgia Technology Systems on 2026-03-27
22836d05-4b01-4dd3-8fef-b4cdd7a949ac.notes:
Notes
Only Nick was on today’s call. I focused on ticketing as he is a project manager.
Payments and Tax Setup
The team is finalizing payment and tax configurations to enable smooth go-live and billing processes.
• Payment integration with Rev IO and TRX processor confirmed (01:02)
    ◦ Customers will pay directly through Rev IO invoices for seamless experience.
    ◦ TRX payment processor offers a lower fee of 1.5% compared to QuickBooks' 3% credit card fee.
    ◦ Mark will coordinate with the payments team to set up payment options and conduct test transactions.
    ◦ Nick emphasized the goal to minimize manual steps for customers during payment.
• Tax code assignment process clarified (43:07)
    ◦ Mark explained the need to assign tax codes to products, with four main product types: non-serialized, serialized, recurring service, and labor.
    ◦ Nick confirmed most products are non-serialized, and Ben will decide the tax codes for each type.
    ◦ SureTax will automatically calculate taxes based on customer location once codes are assigned.
    ◦ Mark will send the tax code list to Nick after the call for final decisions.
• Invoice generation and QuickBooks integration explained (40:57)
    ◦ Invoices created in Rev IO automatically sync to QuickBooks.
    ◦ QuickBooks manages final customer invoice sending, eliminating manual downloads and attachments.
    ◦ Mark showed the billing tabs for managing invoices and confirmed the system supports emailing invoices directly from Rev IO if needed.
    ◦ Nick’s questions confirmed a streamlined workflow between Rev IO and QuickBooks for billing.
Ticketing and Workflow Configuration
The ticketing module setup focuses on efficient ticket creation, assignment, and communication with customers and internal teams.
• Default ticket settings and user groups established (07:35)
    ◦ Service calls and installations are the most common ticket types, roughly split 50/50.
    ◦ A technician user group will be created to pre-populate ticket assignments and improve visibility.
    ◦ Nick suggested including Andrew in the technician group for dispatching.
    ◦ Mark explained default email notification settings and that workflows can automate notifications to technicians.
• Macros introduced to streamline ticket entry (14:29)
    ◦ Macros allow quick filling of repetitive ticket information, such as assigning specific techs to access control projects.
    ◦ Nick plans to track technician assignments using macros for common project types.
    ◦ Mark demonstrated how macros can set ticket type, priority, and assigned techs quickly.
    ◦ Flexibility remains to adjust macros per ticket if needed.
• Internal and public activity notes clarified for communication (19:55)
    ◦ Public notes are visible to customers and can trigger emails; internal notes are for staff only and do not email unless a status triggers workflow.
    ◦ Nick asked about auto-emailing internal notes to a fixed group; Mark confirmed workflows can be built to send emails automatically based on ticket status changes.
    ◦ This setup avoids requiring manual entry of email addresses for routine job reports or updates.
    ◦ Mark emphasized the importance of workflow triggers for notification consistency.
Time Tracking and Field Technician Management
Time logging and technician scheduling features are being set up to track billable hours and coordinate fieldwork efficiently.
• Time logging options and billing rates discussed (27:03)
    ◦ Time can be logged manually or via app start/stop buttons for accuracy.
    ◦ Labor products with different rates (baseline $100–$120/hour, discounted $70–$80/hour) will be set up as separate items for billing.
    ◦ Non-billable time can also be tracked for internal purposes.
    ◦ Nick confirmed use of different rates for recurring and new customers, with Mark advising separate product entries for each.
• Planned GPS tracking feature rollout by June 1 (29:20)
    ◦ Mark shared that GPS tracking for field techs currently isn’t live but will be included in the next PSA update by June 1.
    ◦ The feature will track tech locations during logged hours to improve attendance verification.
    ◦ This addition aligns with operational goals for accurate job site tracking and reporting.
• Task assignment and calendar integration explored (32:12)
    ◦ Tasks can be assigned per ticket with specific time frames and priorities, appearing on the assignee’s calendar.
    ◦ Nick and Mark tested calendar integration with Google; initial sync issues were identified, likely configuration-related.
    ◦ Mark will revisit calendar sync setup in a future call to resolve errors and ensure seamless scheduling.
    ◦ Workflow notifications for assigned tasks were noted as needing setup to automate alerts to techs.
Billing and Dispatch Board Usage
The team reviewed billing finalization and dispatch board features to manage invoicing and technician workloads.
• Billing workflow through Rev IO and QuickBooks confirmed (37:32)
    ◦ Time logs and parts added to tickets flow into billing unposted tab for invoice creation.
    ◦ After invoice creation, billing is handled through QuickBooks, including customer delivery.
    ◦ Mark demonstrated reversing test charges to avoid actual billing during training.
    ◦ Nick confirmed understanding that invoices can be emailed directly from Rev IO or QuickBooks.
• Dispatch board functionality and limitations outlined (40:35)
    ◦ Tickets can be assigned to techs by dragging them on the dispatch board for daily scheduling.
    ◦ The board only shows one day at a time, requiring users to cycle through dates.
    ◦ Closed tickets currently appear by default, but filtering can hide them.
    ◦ Mark expressed desire for improved multi-day view but recognized current limitations.
Next Steps and Scheduling
The team agreed on immediate next steps and future meeting plans to complete setup.
• Immediate priorities identified as tax code and payment setup (42:40)
    ◦ Mark will send tax code assignment instructions to Nick post-call.
    ◦ Payments team will connect with Nick to finalize processor setup and testing.
    ◦ Once these are complete, the system will be ready for live use.
• Follow-up meeting agreed for further training and troubleshooting (48:39)
    ◦ Mark proposed scheduling another session next Friday from 2:00 to 3:00 PM.
    ◦ Nick confirmed Ben’s availability that day to join the call.
    ◦ The next meeting will address unresolved calendar sync issues and continue PSA training.
    ◦ This cadence supports steady progress toward smooth operational adoption.

31ba59b7-e7b2-8159-b1f0-f7c39f4d47c8.title: Call with Bullfrog on 2026-03-25
31ba59b7-e7b2-8159-b1f0-f7c39f4d47c8.notes:
Notes
Ticket Management and Dispatch Automation
The team identified key inefficiencies in ticket handling and agreed on improvements to reduce manual work and streamline assignment.
• Primary technician assignment issue on dispatch board needs fixing (00:04)
    ◦ Currently, dragging a ticket to assign a technician does not update the primary technician field automatically.
    ◦ Michael has to manually edit each ticket to set the primary technician after assignment.
    ◦ Mark Kephart agreed this is not intended behavior and will submit a community request to fix it.
    ◦ A toggle or admin setting to automatically assign the primary tech on task assignment was suggested for flexibility.
• Email ticket threading problem causes duplicate tickets (00:48)
    ◦ When clients reply by email, it creates new tickets instead of appending to the existing thread.
    ◦ This breaks continuity and causes confusion for dispatch and technicians.
    ◦ Chad Pifer and Mark tested and confirmed the issue involves replies sent to a no-reply address.
    ◦ Changing the "from" email to a monitored support address fixed the problem and stopped duplicate tickets.
• Round robin or automated ticket distribution considered but deprioritized (00:02)
    ◦ Chad suggested round robin ticket assignment to reduce manual dispatch work.
    ◦ Mark said this feature does not currently exist but could be requested.
    ◦ The team agreed primary focus is fixing manual assignment issues first.
    ◦ Current workflow requires manual control for balancing workloads, which they want to keep for now.
• Ticket board auto-refresh functionality confirmed (00:51)
    ◦ The ticket dispatch board auto-refreshes approximately every minute to keep information current.
    ◦ This reduces the need for manual page refreshes by technicians monitoring tickets.
    ◦ Ensures timely updates on new or changed tickets without disrupting workflow.
Quote, Invoicing, and Payment Workflow Integration
The meeting clarified how quotes, invoices, and payments flow through the PSA and billing systems to support smooth project onboarding.
• Quote signing and sending process tested successfully (00:07)
    ◦ Chad signed a quote and the system sent notifications to recipients.
    ◦ Mark fixed signature fields for proper formatting in documents.
    ◦ The quote automatically generates follow-up workflows, including purchase orders and work orders.
• Sales tax display issues explained and deferred (00:08)
    ◦ An unusually high tax amount was caused by zip-code-based flat fees plus percentages.
    ◦ Mark explained tax bundling limitations in the PSA system.
    ◦ Ben preferred removing taxes from quotes with a disclaimer instead of complex tax code changes.
    ◦ Integration between billing and PSA is planned to improve by July 1st, which may better handle taxes.
• Invoice creation and payment processing workflow clarified (00:14)
    ◦ Chad demonstrated how to batch create invoices within PSA.
    ◦ Payments can be processed either individually or in bulk by selecting invoices and using the "new action" payment option.
    ◦ Mark confirmed payments do not trigger email notifications but are visible in QuickBooks.
    ◦ Ben planned to run a payment transaction to test the workflow.
• Project creation follows quote and invoice completion (00:18)
    ◦ Mark recommended duplicating existing projects to automatically create corresponding tickets.
    ◦ Ticket creation triggers email workflows notifying assigned engineers or teams.
    ◦ Chad plans to create projects after quote approval and PO creation, then send emails to accounting for billing setup.
    ◦ Integration with Rev Billing remains for customer portal and billing functions, while PSA handles project and ticket management.
Project Setup and Management Best Practices
The team explored the PSA project module’s features and agreed on workflows and access controls tailored to their operational needs.
• Project duplication auto-updates dates and copies phases and tasks (00:29)
    ◦ When duplicating a project, the start date updates to the current date.
    ◦ All milestones, phases, and tasks copy over with relative due dates preserved.
    ◦ This ensures consistent task timelines without manual date adjustments.
• Tasks link to customers and projects for visibility and tracking (00:31)
    ◦ Tickets must be linked to a customer to be added as project tasks.
    ◦ Chad plans to attach tasks to a test customer and then change the customer when duplicating projects.
    ◦ Linking quotes to projects via the files tab improves technician visibility on what was sold and how it was described.
• User access configured for project visibility without editing rights (00:33)
    ◦ Help desk and client success teams get view-only access to projects.
    ◦ This allows technicians to track project progress without editing or deleting content.
    ◦ Mark set standard user permissions accordingly to avoid accidental changes.
• In-app messaging and notifications support project communication (00:34)
    ◦ Messages can be sent within projects, appearing as notifications with priority flags.
    ◦ Chad emphasized the need to include project context in messages for clarity.
    ◦ Mark demonstrated how emergency priority messages highlight in red for urgent attention.
• Proposed project-specific forum or consolidated activity notes (00:39)
    ◦ Chad requested a simple text forum or comment box within projects for team communication.
    ◦ Current system only supports ticket-level activity notes, which fragments communication.
    ◦ Mark agreed to submit this as a feature request to improve knowledge sharing and reduce silos.
    ◦ Alternatively, a single view of all ticket activity notes linked to a project was suggested.
• Archiving projects as final step after completion (00:42)
    ◦ Completed projects can be archived to remove them from active lists.
    ◦ Archived projects remain stored for reference but do not clutter the workspace.
    ◦ Mark confirmed archiving does not automatically complete associated tickets; status changes must be done separately.
Customer Portal and Billing User Experience
The discussion clarified customer portal capabilities and billing user management to improve client interactions.
• Customer portal enables ticket creation, payment, and invoice access (00:45)
    ◦ Customers can open tickets, view ticket lists, make payments, and download invoices via the portal.
    ◦ They can add contacts who become authorized users for ticket creation and notifications.
    ◦ Portal access provides transparency and self-service options for customers.
• Contact roles primarily affect notification recipients (00:46)
    ◦ Contacts can be marked as primary, controlling who receives ticket updates.
    ◦ No distinct permissions limit viewing specific portal sections per contact.
    ◦ Chad requested role-based access to separate billing and technical views, but system currently lacks this granularity.
• Portal user onboarding includes automated welcome emails for password setup (00:47)
    ◦ When creating portal users, customers receive an email prompting them to set their password.
    ◦ This eliminates the need for temporary passwords and manual setup.
    ◦ Mark demonstrated the portal login screen and explained admin controls for tab visibility.
• Admin controls allow enabling or disabling ticket creation in portal (00:47)
    ◦ Turning off ticket creation disables both the ability to create and view tickets in the portal.
    ◦ Chad prefers selective control per user, which is a requested feature by other clients.
    ◦ This limitation may influence how billing and tech contacts are managed.
Workflow Automation and Monitoring Enhancements
The team aligned on configuring workflows and monitoring tools to automate notifications and improve operational efficiency.
• Workflow setup starts after building project with all tickets (00:37)
    ◦ Chad will create workflows triggered by ticket creation to notify assigned technicians like Michael.
    ◦ Mark guided on using workflow conditions and actions for automating email alerts.
    ◦ This standardizes communication and reduces manual follow-ups.
• Integration of project tasks and quotes improves traceability (00:32)
    ◦ Linking quotes and tickets within projects provides quick access to sales details.
    ◦ This helps technicians understand customer expectations and reduces miscommunication.
    ◦ Mark confirmed that standard users can view linked documents and tickets as needed.
• Revi tool provides summarized ticket activity to reduce reading load (00:49)
    ◦ Mark noted the Revie AI summarizes up to 90 days of ticket activity for fast review.
    ◦ This reduces the need to read entire email chains or activity notes.
    ◦ Teams can rely on summaries for efficient ticket handling.
• Email from address issues addressed to improve response routing (00:53)
    ◦ Emails coming from no-reply addresses caused undeliverable replies and new ticket creation.
    ◦ Switching to a monitored support email fixed ticket threading and response workflows.
    ◦ Ensures client replies attach correctly to existing tickets, maintaining conversation continuity.
